title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: root service capability declarations or service-level models depending upward on sealed capability modules through module or binding aliases, provider code reaching current-main through governance module or binding imports instead of the neutral root dependency, CLI access to the retired governance binding, module router bypass of module context, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, package or CLI-local provider-binding value imports outside the native-resource binding and provider-resource composition root, release-binding values outside the artifact/evidence composition root, pure release values outside command parsing, controller release/evidence mechanics outside resource bindings, a second production value consumer of the lifecycle service client, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: Root capability declarations remain independent of sealed governance, packaging, providers, releases, and vendors implementations, and that sealed set grows monotonically as context-direction slices land. Governance owns current-main selection while one closed service-composition middleware derives its neutral observation reader from the raw content-workspace capability; providers consume only that reader through provided context and the CLI cannot construct it. The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and derives provider-domain repositories under service middleware. The root composes module routers through impl.router. Runtime construction narrows through one exact CLI composition root. Provider-transition values enter only at the native-resource binding and provider composition root; transitional artifact/evidence materializers enter only at their composition root; pure release algebra enters command parsing through the release surface.
remediate: Declare root capabilities and cross-module observation contracts under service/model, derive current-main once in service/middleware/current-main.ts from the raw content-workspace capability and governance-owned selector, consume it from service/model/dependencies/current-main through provided context, derive provider repositories and adapters under module provided context, project exact capabilities into each module from module.ts, implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep remaining ports protocol/type-only, construct the lifecycle client only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/client.ts, use type-only lifecycle and provider-binding dependencies elsewhere, import package provider-transition values only at the native-resource binding and provider composition root, import release-binding materializers only at the artifact/evidence composition root, import pure release parsers directly from the release surface in commands/input.ts, and keep release/evidence mechanics behind resource contracts.
message: Lifecycle dependencies flow from root-owned capabilities and neutral cross-module observations into sealed module contexts and handlers, then through impl.router and exact CLI composition roots; pure release algebra does not flow through host bindings.
